subject: Databases
title: GIN Indexes for Full-Text Search
description: One-minute skill: GIN Indexes for Full-Text Search.

hook.text: Search for articles containing 'database' and 'performance'. A GIN index on the text column finds them fast.

buildup.text: GIN (Generalized Inverted Index) maps each word to the rows that contain it. Like a book's index.

discovery.text: Postgres tsvector converts text into searchable tokens. GIN indexes tsvector columns for fast full-text search.

twist.text: GIN indexes are large and slow to update. Great for read-heavy text search, costly for write-heavy tables.

climax.text: For many apps, Postgres full-text search with GIN replaces Elasticsearch entirely. One fewer service to run.

punchline.text: GIN: full-text search inside Postgres. No external engine needed.

quiz.question: What does a GIN index map in full-text search?
options: Each word or token to the list of rows that contain it
Each row to a single keyword
The table schema to a search engine
